title: oxalis tomentosa
intro: Oxalis tomentosa, also known as the woolly woodsorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family. It is native to South America and has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic and to reduce inflammation. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Oxalis tomentosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies to aid in digestion and to promote overall well-being.
